commit.message:
Remove unnecessary trie bumping from Document.__or__

__or__ returns a brand-new Document with no outstanding proxies, so
bumping replaced keys in the trie is a no-op.  Removing it makes
Document.__or__ consistent with DictProxy.__or__, which already
discarded the replaced-keys list.

commit.message:
Fix AoT __setitem__ to convert inline tables to tables

item_setitem_int for ArrayOfTables assigned the value directly via
indexing, bypassing the InlineTable→Table conversion that append,
insert, and extend all perform via require_table(). This corrupted
the AoT's internal structure when assigning an inline-table proxy,
producing invalid TOML output.

Use require_table() + remove/insert to go through the proper AoT API,
matching the pattern used by the other list mutation methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix AppHost.Tests CI failures: add health check wait after Aspire startup

AppHost.Tests were failing in CI with:
- redis_check timeout (5000ms) and connection refused
- web_https_/health_200_check timeout with connection refused on localhost:7043

Root cause: AspireManager.StartAppAsync() returned immediately after App.StartAsync()
without waiting for Redis and Web services to become healthy. This caused:
1. EnvVarTests to read env vars before Redis was ready
2. Subsequent E2E tests to fail on connection timeouts

Fix: Add WaitForWebHealthyAsync() which polls the /health endpoint (using a
certificate-ignoring HttpClient for self-signed HTTPS in CI) until it returns 2xx.
Since AppHost.cs configures Web to WaitFor(redis), the health check ensures both
Redis and Web are ready before tests begin.

The wait uses a 120-second timeout to accommodate CI cold-start (30-60s for Redis).
Local dev typically succeeds within ~10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Boromir: Document AppHost.Tests Aspire startup fix learnings

Documented the investigation and fix for AppHost.Tests CI flakiness caused by

commit.message:
Fix message sent to wrong chat when switching during worktree creation

The bug: when a user sends a message in a new worktree chat and switches
to another chat before CreateWorktreeAsync completes, the message and
its streaming response end up in the wrong chat.

Root cause: SendMessage() used CurrentChat (a mutable property) and
_activeSession (a shared field) throughout the method. Both get
overwritten when LoadChatAsync runs for the chat the user switches to.
The streaming event handlers gate UI updates on _activeSession == session,
so when EnsureSessionAsync overwrites _activeSession for the background
chat, the response pollutes the displayed chat's UI.

Fix:
1. Capture CurrentChat into a local 'targetChat' before the async
   worktree creation gap and use it for all Chat model operations.
2. After EnsureSessionAsync sets up the session for targetChat, capture
   it as 'sendSession' and call RestoreActiveSessionIfSwitched to point
   _activeSession back to the displayed chat's session. This ensures
   streaming events for the background send don't touch the UI.
3. Use sendSession for CaptureExpectedSessionUserMessageCountAsync and
   SendAsync calls, including retry paths.
4. Update HandleSendError to accept an explicit Chat parameter so error
   messages target the correct chat. Guard view-level property updates
   behind CurrentChat identity checks.
5. Fix TryRecoverTransportSendAsync to look up the session from
   _sessionCache instead of using _activeSession, which may have been
   restored to the displayed chat.
6. Extract RestoreActiveSessionIfSwitched helper and call it after every
   code path that sets _activeSession for a background chat (session
   setup, retry, transport recovery).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix streaming text getting stuck during chat responses

Three root causes fixed:

1. UiThrottler dispatched flushes at DispatcherPriority.Background,
   which got starved by higher-priority layout/render work. Changed
   default to DispatcherPriority.Normal.

2. UiThrottler and StrataMarkdown had no error recovery in their
   async scheduling paths — an unexpected exception would leave
   _scheduled/_rebuildQueued stuck as true forever, permanently
   blocking all future updates.

3. The session event handler (~1000 lines) had no try-catch wrapper.
   If any case handler threw, the SDK would stop delivering events,
   leaving IsStreaming/IsBusy stuck permanently with no error shown.

Added 10 regression tests covering priority ordering, starvation
resistance, cancel+request patterns, and StrataMarkdown streaming.

commit.message:
fix: prevent DataStore.Save() deadlock on STA thread during startup seeding

Task.Run() pushes SaveAsync() to a threadpool thread, avoiding the classic
STA deadlock where .GetResult() blocks the main thread while the async
continuation tries to post back to it.
